seo.title: How We Score Ideas | Fly Labs
seo.description: The Ideas Lab finds real problems from {{sourceCount}} sources, asks {{questionCount}} questions, validates against X and Reddit, and gives a verdict. Here's how.

hero.title: How We Score Ideas
hero.subtitle: {{sourceCount}} sources, {{questionCount}} questions, one verdict.
hero.description: The Ideas Lab pulls problems from {{sourceCount}} sources, researches the market on X and Reddit, scores them through the Fly Labs Method, and tells you: build it, validate it, or skip it.

steps.researchAndScoreDesc: Search X and Reddit for evidence, map competitors, then score with AI

cycle.description: The Ideas Lab covers Stage 1 of the vibe building cycle: finding problems worth solving. More stages coming.

method.title: {{count}} Questions Before You Write a Line of Code
method.description: The Fly Labs Method evaluates every idea from the perspective of a solo builder with AI tools and limited time. {{count}} questions, 100 points. That's it.

questions.title: The {{count}} questions
questions.subtitle: Each idea gets scored on these questions. The total is the FL score (0-100). That's the only score that decides the verdict.
questions.q1What: Does this problem actually exist? Are people aware of it? How bad does it hurt?
questions.q2: Is there room for something new?
questions.q2What: What already exists? Where do current solutions fall short? Is there whitespace?
questions.q3: Would people actually do something about it?
questions.q3What: Are people frustrated enough to switch? Would they pay? Is there urgency?
questions.q4What: Can one person build this with AI tools and limited time? How fast? Does it compound?

buildersNote.label: Builder's Note
buildersNote.text: I built this because the hardest part of building is knowing WHAT to build. You can have the skills, the tools, the time on a Saturday morning. But if you pick the wrong problem, none of that matters. These {{count}} questions are the filter I wish I had before I started.

validationSection.title: Then We Check the Real World
validationSection.description: The scoring step does homework first. Before giving a grade, we search X for real complaints and competitor discussions, and Reddit for community evidence. All that research feeds into the score.
validationSection.evidenceDesc: We search X and Reddit for real people experiencing this pain. Tweets with engagement, Reddit threads with upvotes and comments, frustration language from actual users.
validationSection.competitiveDesc: Who else is solving this? What do they charge? Where do people complain about them? We map the landscape from real conversations, not marketing pages.
validationSection.confidenceDesc: AI synthesizes everything with a confidence level (high, medium, low based on evidence volume), recurring themes, unmet needs, and a final take that cross-references scores with market reality.

verdicts.title: One Answer
verdicts.description: The Fly Labs Method answers {{questionCount}} questions. That score decides the verdict. {{expertCount}} expert perspectives add depth on the detail page but they don't change the number. When market validation exists, real evidence informs confidence. You get one of three answers.
verdicts.buildDesc: FL score 65 or higher and you can actually build it. Strong signal. Worth your Saturday morning.
verdicts.validateDesc: FL score 40 to 64. Promising but something's off. Talk to real people before you build.
verdicts.skipDesc: FL score below 40. The numbers say move on. There are more ideas in the lab.

validation.title: Validation Layer
validation.description: Top-scoring ideas get validated against real conversations on X and Reddit.

experts.title: Want more depth?
experts.subtitle: {{count}} expert perspectives add angles the FL score doesn't cover. They live on each idea's detail page but don't affect the verdict.
experts.description: {{count}} expert perspectives (Hormozi, Dan Koe, Okamoto, YC Lens) add depth on the detail page. They do not affect the score or verdict.
experts.disclaimer: These frameworks were adapted from public teachings by each author. They add depth to the Fly Labs Method and help catch blind spots. The original authors are not affiliated with Fly Labs.
experts.hormoziTagline: Wrote the book on offer design. His Value Equation reframes what makes people buy.
experts.hormoziDisclaimer: Inspired by $100M Offers and related content. Adapted for solo builders.
experts.koeTagline: Built a one-person business proving you don't need a team or a niche. His framework checks whether a solo creator can actually ship and monetize.
experts.koeDisclaimer: Inspired by Dan Koe's public content on one-person businesses. Adapted for idea evaluation.
experts.okamotoTagline: Built 3 companies and Brazil's largest MicroSaaS community (20,000+ founders). Stress-tests distribution and business model assumptions.
experts.okamotoDisclaimer: Inspired by Bruno Okamoto's MicroSaaS validation methodology. Adapted for vibe builders.
experts.ycTagline: Distilled from how Y Combinator evaluates products. 6 forcing questions that separate vitamins from painkillers.
experts.ycDisclaimer: Inspired by Garry Tan, Michael Seibel, and YC's public evaluation methodology. Not affiliated with Y Combinator.
experts.ycPillar1Desc: Would someone be genuinely upset if this product disappeared tomorrow?
experts.ycPillar2Desc: What are users doing today to solve this badly? What does it cost them?
experts.ycPillar3Desc: Can you name the actual human who needs this most?
experts.ycPillar4Desc: Smallest version someone would pay for THIS WEEK?
experts.ycPillar5Desc: Evidence of real usage? What surprised people?
experts.ycPillar6Desc: In 3 years, more essential or less?

dyor.title: Do Your Own Research
dyor.text: AI scores are a starting point, not the finish line. They catch patterns and save you hours of research, but they can't talk to your potential users, understand your specific context, or feel the problem the way you can. Use the scores to narrow the field, then do the work only a human can do: talk to people, test assumptions, build a prototype. The best ideas still need a builder who cares enough to figure out the last mile.

cta.subtitle: Every idea scored, validated, and given a verdict. Go find the signal.
